tasks.name: Extend event_status_enum with academic event lifecycle states
tasks.description: Modify the existing event_status_enum to include all academic event lifecycle states as specified in events_plan.md (Draft, Published, Abstract submission Open, Abstract Review, Full Paper submission Open, Full Paper Review, Completed, Canceled).
tasks.notes: The current event_status_enum has 'published', 'active', 'completed', 'canceled', 'draft' but doesn't have the academic-specific states like 'Abstract submission Open', 'Abstract Review', etc.

relatedFiles.description: New migration script to alter event_status_enum

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that alters the existing event_status_enum type to add the new states. Ensure the migration handles potential existing data properly and doesn't break existing functionality. Test the enum with sample queries after modification.
tasks.verificationCriteria: The event_status_enum type in the database includes all required states from events_plan.md. Sample queries using the new enum values work correctly.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the analysis of events_plan.md and the memory bank files, I've planned the implementation of the Academic Events feature for Eventy360. This feature will support the complete lifecycle of academic event management, including event creation, submission management, review processes, and participant interactions. The implementation will follow the project's Arabic-first approach, integrate with the existing subscription system, and ensure proper security through RLS policies.
tasks.summary: Task successfully completed. Created a new migration script that extends the event_status_enum with all academic event lifecycle states as specified in events_plan.md. The migration properly handles existing data, includes safety checks to avoid duplicates, and adds detailed comments. Also updated the TypeScript type definitions to match the new enum values, ensuring full type safety throughout the application.

tasks.name: Add academic event deadline fields to events table
tasks.description: Extend the events table with academic-specific deadline fields as specified in events_plan.md: abstract_submission_deadline, abstract_review_result_date, full_paper_submission_deadline, revision_deadline (optional).
tasks.notes: The events table already has some deadline fields like submission_deadline, submission_verdict_deadline, full_paper_deadline, but may need additional ones specifically for the academic event workflow.

relatedFiles.description: Migration script to add deadline fields to events table

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that adds the new deadline fields to the events table. All fields should be of type timestamp with time zone and can be nullable except for mandatory deadlines. Update existing constraints to ensure logical date sequence (event_date ≥ submission_verdict_deadline ≥ full_paper_submission_deadline ≥ abstract_review_result_date ≥ abstract_submission_deadline).
tasks.verificationCriteria: The events table contains all the deadline fields needed for academic events. Constraints ensure logical date sequence. Sample inserts and updates with the new fields work correctly.

The implementation will follow the project's Arabic-first approach, integrate with the existing subscription system, and ensure proper security through RLS policies.

tasks.name: Create submission_versions table for version tracking
tasks.description: Create a new table 'submission_versions' to track different versions of submissions (abstracts and full papers) as researchers revise their work, supporting the version control requirement in events_plan.md.
tasks.notes: This table will enable the versioning system described in events_plan.md, where each abstract or paper can have multiple versions with feedback linked to specific versions.

relatedFiles.description: Migration script to create submission_versions table

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that defines the submission_versions table with fields for id (uuid), submission_id (foreign key to submissions), version_number (integer), type (abstract/full_paper), file_url (text), file_metadata (jsonb), submitted_at (timestamp with time zone), feedback (text or jsonb for translations), created_at, and updated_at. Include appropriate indexes and constraints.
tasks.verificationCriteria: The submission_versions table exists with all required fields and appropriate constraints. Sample inserts and queries work as expected.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the analysis of events_plan.md and the memory bank files, I've planned the implementation of the Academic Events feature for Eventy360. The implementation will follow the project's Arabic-first approach, integrate with the existing subscription system, and ensure proper security through RLS policies.
tasks.summary: The submission_versions table has been successfully implemented in migration file 20250526083423_create_submission_versions.sql. The implementation includes all required fields (id, submission_id, version_number, title_translations, abstract_translations, file URLs, metadata, etc.), proper indexes, row-level security policies, and a trigger to automatically increment version numbers. The schema meets all requirements specified in events_plan.md.

tasks.name: Configure storage for submission files with proper RLS
tasks.description: Set up the storage structure for submission files as defined in events_plan.md, with proper organization by event_id, submission_id, type (abstract/full_paper), and version. Implement RLS policies to ensure appropriate access control.
tasks.notes: The storage structure should follow the pattern in events_plan.md: submission_files/{event_id}/{submission_id}/{abstract|full_paper}/v{n}/{filename}. Access control is critical for maintaining submission privacy and security.

relatedFiles.description: Migration script to set up storage for submission files

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that sets up a new storage bucket 'submission_files' with the structure described in events_plan.md. Implement RLS policies to ensure researchers can only access their own submissions, organizers can access submissions for their events, and admins can view all submissions. Add appropriate indexes and constraints.

tasks.name: Implement automated event status updates based on deadlines

tasks.notes: The function should compare the current date with each deadline and update the event status following the progression: Published → Abstract submission Open → Abstract Review → Full Paper submission Open → Full Paper Review → Completed.

relatedFiles.description: Migration script for event status update function and trigger
Edge Function for scheduled status updates (if using Edge Functions approach)

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that defines a PostgreSQL function 'update_event_status_based_on_deadlines()' to check event deadlines and update status accordingly. Set up a trigger or scheduled function (via Supabase Edge Functions) to run this check periodically. The function should handle all status transitions in the academic event lifecycle.
tasks.verificationCriteria: The automated status update function correctly transitions events through lifecycle stages based on the current date relative to deadlines. Test cases with various deadline configurations work as expected.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the analysis of events_plan.md and the memory bank files, I've planned the implementation of the Academic Events feature for Eventy360. tasks.summary: The 'update_event_status_based_on_deadlines' function has been created and a scheduling mechanism (either pg_cron or Supabase Edge Function) is assumed to be configured by the user as per instructions. This function will handle the automated transitions of event statuses based on their deadlines.

tasks.name: Create functions for submission status transitions
tasks.description: Implement database functions to handle submission status transitions according to the workflow in events_plan.md, ensuring proper validation and authorization.
tasks.notes: These functions will enforce the submission workflow rules and ensure that transitions only happen in the allowed sequence with proper authorization. They should work with the submission_versions table to track version history.

relatedFiles.description: Migration script for submission status transition functions

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that defines PostgreSQL functions for transitions like submit_abstract, review_abstract, submit_full_paper, request_revision, etc. Each function should validate the current status, user role, and other constraints before making the transition. Include proper error handling and return appropriate values.
tasks.verificationCriteria: The submission status transition functions enforce the correct workflow. Test cases for valid and invalid transitions work as expected. Error messages are appropriate and informative.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the analysis of events_plan.md and the memory bank files, I've planned the implementation of the Academic Events feature for Eventy360. The implementation will follow the project's Arabic-first approach, integrate with the existing subscription system, and ensure proper security through RLS policies.
tasks.summary: I've implemented comprehensive database functions for submission status transitions according to the academic workflow defined in events_plan.md. The implementation includes five key functions: submit_abstract, review_abstract, submit_full_paper, review_full_paper, and submit_revision, plus a complete_submission function to finalize the process. Each function includes proper validation of the current status, user authorization checks, deadline enforcement, and detailed tracking of submission history. The functions maintain a complete audit trail through the feedback_history field and create appropriate version records in the submission_versions table. All transitions follow the specified workflow with appropriate error messages for invalid operations.

tasks.name: Implement soft delete system for events and submissions
tasks.description: Create a soft delete system for events and submissions with a 7-day grace period as specified in events_plan.md.
tasks.notes: The soft delete system allows for accidental deletion recovery while eventually cleaning up deleted data. This is especially important for academic events where data integrity is critical.

relatedFiles.description: Migration script for soft delete system
Edge Function for permanent deletion after grace period

tasks.implementationGuide: Create a migration script that adds deleted_at timestamp fields to events and submissions tables, and implements functions for soft delete and restore operations. Create a scheduled function to permanently delete items after the 7-day grace period. Update RLS policies and queries to exclude soft-deleted items by default.
tasks.verificationCriteria: The soft delete system works correctly for events and submissions. Soft-deleted items are excluded from normal queries but can be restored within the grace period. After the grace period, they are permanently deleted.
tasks.analysisResult: Based on the analysis of events_plan.md and the memory bank files, I've planned the implementation of the Academic Events feature for Eventy360. This feature will support the complete lifecycle of academic event management, including event creation, submission management, review processes, and participant interactions. The implementation will follow the project's Arabic-first approach, integrate with the existing subscription system, and ensure proper security through RLS policies.
tasks.summary:
I have successfully implemented a soft delete system for events and submissions with a 7-day grace period as specified in events_plan.md. The implementation includes:

1. Database migration (20250601000000_implement_soft_delete_system.sql) that adds deleted_at timestamp fields to both tables
2. Helper functions for soft delete and restore operations with proper authorization checks
3. Updated RLS policies to exclude soft-deleted items from regular queries
4. Specialized RLS policies to allow owners and admins to view their soft-deleted items
5. A purge_expired_deletions() database function to permanently delete items after the 7-day grace period
6. An Edge Function (purge-deleted-items) scheduled to run daily to execute the purge operation
7. Modified existing query functions to exclude soft-deleted items

The system allows recovery of accidentally deleted content within the grace period while maintaining data integrity and security.

tasks.summary: Task successfully completed. Created comprehensive event discovery and filtering functions including get_featured_events, search_events, filter_events_by_topic, filter_events_by_location, filter_events_by_date_range, discover_events, and get_events_by_status. All functions respect the Arabic-first approach with proper JSONB queries on translatable fields using the 'ar' key. The functions include proper indexing for performance optimization and integrate well with the existing RLS system. Additionally, the missing status column issue was resolved by adding the status column to the events table with proper event_status_enum type and default values.
